paper_overview.citation: Verma, N., Singh, G., & Ahsan, N. (2023). Assessment of Spatiotemporal Variations in Water Quality of the Urban River Reach, Yamuna, Delhi. Water Air Soil Pollut, 234(571). https://doi.org/10.1007/s11270-023-06569-1
paper_overview.study_region: Urban River Reach, Yamuna, Delhi, India

data_sources.location: Real-Time Water Quality Monitoring Stations (RTWQMS) of the Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) at Wazirabad and Okhla
data_sources.description: Hourly and 15-min interval data for 11 water quality parameters collected from March 2021 to February 2022 using S::CAN water monitoring technologies.

sites.description: Wazirabad barrage, upstream monitoring station on Yamuna River, Delhi
sites.source_quote: The data were collected from two Real-Time Water Quality Monitoring Stations (RTWQMS) of the Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) upstream of the Wazirabad (ST1) barrage and upstream of the Okhla (ST2) barrage. ... The urban reach of the Yamuna, Delhi, is located between Wazirabad (28°49′24.39"N and 28°31′50.99″ N) and Okhla (77°13′39.92" E and 77°20′36.8" E)

sites.description: Okhla barrage, upstream monitoring station on Yamuna River, Delhi
sites.source_quote: The data were collected from two Real-Time Water Quality Monitoring Stations (RTWQMS) of the Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) upstream of the Wazirabad (ST1) barrage and upstream of the Okhla (ST2) barrage. ... The urban reach of the Yamuna, Delhi, is located between Wazirabad (28°49′24.39"N and 28°31′50.99″ N) and Okhla (77°13′39.92

temporal_coverage.sampling_dates: Data collected from March 2021 to February 2022. For ST1, 6085 hourly observations. For ST2, 25,540 observations at 15-min intervals.

extraction_notes: Coordinates for ST1 and ST2 were inferred from the range provided for the urban reach of the Yamuna River, with Wazirabad being the upstream point and Okhla the downstream point. pH unit 's.u.' is interpreted as standard unit.
